Gu Quan never spoke a word about that child's whereabouts during the decade he spent in Baizao, right up until his death...
I'm sure you're now well aware of why he died on the road back to Victoria.
Yu Cheng, you old bastard.
You used Gu Quan's own death to ensure there was no possibility of anyone finding that child, which also gave you an opportunity to investigate me.
At the time I assumed Gu Quan had no success in his exhaustive research into the Grand Preceptor case, which prompted him to leave Baizao in despair.
It was only after many years that I realized it was not the so-called truth that he sought. He had long since found the answers to those troubles you mentioned earlier.
What he hoped I would do was something else entirely.
I don't understand...
Are you telling me you two knew all along that Blaze was the orphan from that case?
Your investigation should have already revealed that Gu Quan and Yu Cheng knew each other.
After taking the child away, Gu Quan kept the secret of her whereabouts to himself for decades. But before he met his untimely end, he confided in the only 'friend' he had, Yu Cheng.
After his death, Yu Cheng immediately began to search for her.

However, he was unable to personally act as deputy minister at the Judicial Review Office. He entrusted the task of performing discreet inquiries to me, far from the center of court politics, so as to not attract attention.
By the time I found her in Victoria, she was already infected.
I could not take her back to Baizao right away. For the sake of protecting her, I had no choice but to place her in the care of a medical organization that treated the Infected.
So it was you...
You were already aligned at the time, I see.
But you were also part of this plan from early on, Deputy Minister Lin.
What do you mean?
How was it you were able to find Blaze's trail? How were you able to come across those files pertaining to her past? Surely you didn't just attribute them to chance?
Yu Cheng would not have told you these himself. The risk was too great. There could not be a third person who knew of this in all of Terra.
But he was always searching for a suitable candidate... One with courage, who stood for truth.
He has long since been ready to give his life for this cause. I am happy to dirty my hands doing what must be done in his stead.
But it's not enough. We needed someone pure and innocent, one who could use honest means to lay bare the truth.
It's nearly time.
We still have one last opportunity.
Do you still wish to bring the truth out into the light of day, Deputy Minister Lin?
Yu Cheng... That is the truth behind the Grand Preceptor case you seek.
Do you have any further questions?
You've spoken at length, yet you've said nothing about your role in these events, Mr. Ning.
Why did you send someone to kill the Minister of Justice?
Do you not already have the answer, Deputy Minister Yu? Was it not you who accused me years ago of having the Minister murdered to stop him from investigating and save myself in the chaos?
You need not be in such a hurry to discredit yourself.
There was one question that perplexed me for a long time. Why did Gu Quan stay at your house when he returned to Baizao nearly a decade later?
It was not until I saw that girl at your house did all the puzzle pieces fall into place.
He narrowly escaped with his life, yet he still returned to Baizao, not only to find the truth of what happened, but also to search for the daughter he left behind.
It seems he did not tell you much.
Yes. Most of what follows will be mere speculation. If I'm wrong, feel free to laugh in my face.

I heard that after the Grand Preceptor's suicide following accusations of treason, the entire nation was in uproar and mandarins distanced themselves from her. Yet, one Academy student dared to honor her in public.

It was thus that he stumbled onto the Grand Preceptor's home as the belongings within were being confiscated. In his hurry, all he could take away was a baby girl, just born into the family. As it so happened, his own daughter was recently born as well.
At his wit's end, he tried to flee Baizao with his wife and the two babies. As a scholar with no background, he didn't get far before an Imperial Guard captured him.
Perhaps out of compassion and wanting to keep the Grand Preceptor's bloodline going, the Imperial Guard spared the scholar and the Grand Preceptor's child, instead taking the scholar's daughter.
I do not know what Gu Quan was thinking at the time, but it was most likely he had no other choice.

This child fell into the hands of the Minister of Justice, her bloodline obvious with a modicum of research. But as it turned out, how to handle this child of an outsider was a delicate conundrum indeed.
It was around this time that you, Mr. Ning, long dissatisfied with the Minister's extreme methods, decided to take the ultimate risk: assassination.

The Grand Preceptor's case implicated many, and it seemed fitting that the bloodstained Minister of Justice met his end through retribution. With the court's turmoil resolved, the True Lung allowed the bloodstained curtain to finally fall in this fashion.
The only unresolved question left is that child. Based on how things are now, she was taken into your care. What this entailed is not hard to guess. But as for the why of it...
If I recall correctly, Mr. Ning, you enjoyed the tutelage of the Grand Preceptor as well, correct?
O Yu Cheng, had you not been absorbed in Judicial work or confined to this cell, I shudder to think what that meticulous mind of yours might have accomplished.
It's terrifying, truly terrifying.

You flatter me. I'm nothing more than a fool compared to Gu Quan. It is simply because I have met far too many people in my capacity as Deputy Minister of the Judicial Review Office. The more one sees, the more one learns.
Now that I think about it, the dish Gu Quan offered to the True Lung at the Feast of a Hundred Delicacies wasn't merely an act of suicide—it was him reconciling with the truth.

Before he passed, he did not tell me the truth, but he did divulge the whereabouts of the Grand Preceptor's orphan. He must have hoped that once I found the truth for myself, I would also choose reconciliation.
Yet I could not.
All I could do was use my methods to find the people still left, the ones you care for, and force you to spit out the truth.
All things considered, Mr. Ning, you are no villain, perhaps just a coward...
Hate me all you want.
To see that child once more has always been a wish of mine...
I will go and seek an audience with His Majesty.
You... You know everything?
I only just found out...
I still want to ask you—
How are you... so calm about all this...?
It was a suspicion I hid away in my heart for many, many years... One I have finally found proof for.
Like a thorn stuck in your palm. It may bleed a bit when you pluck it out, but you'll feel much better afterward.
Why...
Why won't you say something?
I don't know how it came to all this...
Should I feel sad or angry right now...?
I don't know... All I know is that my mind's a mess and my heart feels like it's being stabbed... Who the hell am I supposed to set ablaze with this fire crawling up my throat?!
Well, I have a question for you.
Blaze... Can you tell me about Gu Quan? My... my father. What sort of man was he?
He... He was an idiot, a nerd, and a liar!
I hate lies! I've seen too damn many of them! Greedy lies, cowardly lies, sneaky lies...
But never one that demanded a lifetime of commitment, one that cost him his own daughter and himself, all to protect someone who had nothing to do with him.
How... How could he be so stupid?
It reminds me of a story I once read...
There once was a guard who watched over a rest station by the river. One day, he saw a flood coming from upstream. He wanted to run upstream and tell the people there to close the sluice gates.
But he had only run halfway when he saw the river was rising too quickly. It was too late to close the gates. So he immediately decided to turn and warn the villagers downstream to flee.
Back and forth he ran, until finally he was consumed and swept away by the flood. He didn't manage to do anything or save anyone.
Get to the point already...
Would you... Would you say this man's deeds sound familiar?
Fine, I get it! Well, I don't really get all the details of your windy-ass story, but you're talking shit about him, aren't you?
He wasn't a bad guy, okay?! He was just... Urgh!
I know you've got good reasons to blame him, duh, but if he was the one who threw you away, why would he spend so much time coming back to look for you—
I thought I would be the one consoling you, not the other way around...
Blaze... You're infected, right?
Wait, when did you figure that out—
Man, you're too... you're too damn clever by half!
Sorry, I didn't mean anything untoward... I was just thinking that it really should have been my fate to be...
The hell are you even talking about?!
What? You think you've done something wrong and deserve a worse fate?
Please, my life is awesome. I've got lots of buddies, and I've found a dream of mine...
If anything, you're the one who got it bad! Growing up all by yourself in that big old mansion, alone and miserable.
It almost sounds like you're roasting me...
I'm not—
But I understand... I understand what you mean.
Oh, right... I still haven't asked you what your name actually is.
Zhuhuang... Gu Zhuhuang. It's the name Dad... he gave me.
Wow... I forget how long it's been since I last told someone my real name.
Well, that's it. That's the last of my secrets.
'A blazing candle to ward against the dark'... Zhu for a candle, Huang for a blaze, and the family name Gu to ward you. It really does sound like a name he would pick.
Blaze... Would you say I've only really gotten to know you now, after all this?
I... I guess so, yeah.
Wait, no, I've still got lots to tell you, and I need to bring you to Victoria to have a look around. My mom—err, or rather... your birth mother. You should probably go see her...
...She's still alive?
Who said she was dead?! She's still hale and hearty! She lives in a small town out in western Victoria.
Once everything's wrapped up here, I'll take you to her.
Sh-She definitely wants to see you...
A-Alright...
Why're you crying?
I-I'm not...
But you... You're the one who's about to break into tears any second now...
I'm just... happy.
I never thought I'd suddenly find myself a little sister after all these years.
Who's ever heard of sisters like us...?
And besides, we don't know yet who's the little one.
I've been through a lot today. Can't you let me have this one?
And there you were, consoling me...
Look, I just wanted to say...
From now on, you're not alone anymore, okay?
Man... Isn't that lovely?
Out of the myriad flavors in this land, the taste of reunion is the sweetest of them all.
Really, now... Dammit, what am I tearing up for...
That's the last of my worries settled.
Oh, right, that Judicial officer at the Forbidden City last night...
Leizi...
She was being held up by that Imperial Guard lady when she sent me flying out of the City.
What should we do now?
This isn't over quite yet...
I need to go and finish off what I should have done in the first place.
You... What are you planning to do?
Demand an explanation... for everyone's sake.
One hundred years it has been.
He returns once again, to where this game of weiqi started.
The board is covered in cobwebs and dust, a single black stone at the center.
He extends his hand to brush away the grime.
Where you off to?
Got a meal to deliver.
If you're going, then who's going to mind the restaurant?
At this hour? It's past closing time.
Gonna open tomorrow?
That depends on my mood tomorrow. Hard to say right now.
Why do I get the feeling that you want to call it quits?
Fine by me, but you still owe me six months of wages. Settle that and I'll let you go.
Tsch... You're so money-minded! How about all those staff meals I cooked for you, they must be worth a few months of debt...
Fine, fine... On the right of the stove in the kitchen, the third brick from the corner. The last of my spare cash is under that brick. Take it.
I've been with you for quite a few years now.
Yep, fifteen whole years. You've stuck around the longest out of all your associates, Candleholder. Means we were really fated to be, haha.
If I kept reminding myself that I was a Candleholder of the Sui Regulator, I'm afraid I wouldn't have stayed that long.
*sigh* To be honest, it's been years since I reminded myself who I really am too.
Not like I was trying to remember or not, mind you. Whether as a man or beast, I've found this life full of delightful flavors. Nothing to complain about.
Best you keep not remembering.
I fear if you do, you'll forget the tastes of man. Your palate will grow dull and numb, and your cooking skills will go to waste.
*sigh*... Yeah, I wouldn't be too happy with that either.
But if there's one thing I'll never forget, it's that my trouble-making brothers and sisters still need to eat.
Not to mention the one that's most troublesome of all... Though considering how long it's been since we last saw each other, it's hard to say what he's like now.
You know the consequences of delivering this particular meal today, right?
Uh... Yeah, more or less.
It's obvious, like not keeping cotton by the stove. The Sui Regulator's not going to be happy if we have a family reunion.
Also, if a fight breaks out at such a delicate moment, you might not be seeing me tomorrow.
*sigh*... I've made a lot of food and met a lot of people these past few years.
My wish was for every customer who walks through those doors to have a tasty meal and a life with fewer regrets.
The four flavors of the kitchen. The four emotions of the heart. Sorrowful partings and joyful reunions. They're not that much different.
But just as fresh fins have their bones and maluses have no scent, this land is filled with regrets.
Eat when you're hungry, sleep when you're tired... and when you miss your family, maybe it's as simple as going to them.
Are... Are you really willing to let this go...
There are a lot of things I don't want to let go of, but I can't hold onto them forever either.
Jiang... Mind looking after the shop for me while I'm gone?
You've been watching me cook for so long, you must have picked something up by now. If any customers come by, look after them for me, will you?
'Savors at Yu's', the 'House of Aftertaste'... It ended up being a fitting name.
The mortal realm really lingers on the tongue.
Captain, my report. The Patrol Battalion has dispatched all their men, and sweep searches are underway in eight districts. Still no sign of the target.
It's been an entire day...
Before day breaks, we must—
Heard you were looking for me?
Who's there?!
Out of the way.
What's this? Here to surrender?
Good, you've saved me the effort of searching the entire city for you.
I don't care if you want to take me in for what I did at the Feast or who I am... Now's not the time for it.
I've got urgent business, so best you move it.
Hah...
I've never been this pissed off before. If you really want to get in my way, then come and try me.
Hmph...
...What are you going to do?
I'm here to deliver a bowl of longevity noodles to the True Lung.

lines.st: Victoria is so close...
It's snowing again... they should be wearing their winter garb now.
She was like a little stove as a child, aflame with passion in her veins. This snow and wind won't faze her.
Baizao winters are never this cold. She said the first volume should be done when spring comes.
That's good...
At life's end, I find no worries left to bear.

lines.x: The man takes off his mandarin garb, folds it carefully. and places it down next to the books he used to carry, before bowing three times to the east.
He walks out of the camp.
Your flames are indeed hotter now.
Your Arts are greatly burdening your body, Infected.
Scared of the heat?
Heh, it's just my blood... Though I know I'm standing here only because so many others have shed their own blood.
Seems like you already know a lot.
Not much at all, but there's just one thing I need to know—
Those who had their names erased, those who died dubious deaths... they need an explanation, an answer.
To be honest, I didn't think my life would have such a complicated backstory, or that I'd fight for something other than the Infected.
But now I realize that injustice is the same for everyone. And I will spill my blood to end every injustice I encounter.
Burning a blaze with your blood... Infected, you resemble the righteous of old.
But don't rush to conclusions about justice when a single leaf blocks your view of the entire forest.
In any case, I have been ordered to bring you back, and you absolutely cannot be let free...
I know, I know... I've met your type before, plenty of times.
I guess I can't paint you all as bad guys. You've just been trained to shed no blood or tears... so you stop caring about anyone else's.
Whatever. I won't argue—you sure can try to stop me though.
You are the enemy... but your courage deserves praise.
Careful now. I'll spare your life, but I can't guarantee you won't lose a limb or two.
Zongguan Bailei Zhengfa, cleave!
It's you...
I know you have a lot of questions, but there's only one pressing matter at hand.
You wanted to see the True Lung, yes? I'm here to help.
Lin Qingyan? You should be behind bars!
Yet I stand here before you now.
You've already lost to me once. This time, neither of you will escape.
Don't worry, I have no plans to fight you alone.
I know I asked to tag along...
But this opponent... will be hard to handle.
An accomplice, even...
So you were the one who freed her from prison...
I had to employ some crude measures, but I tried my best to avoid harming any innocents.
Don't worry about settling the score. I have too many crimes I can't escape from, and this is just one more. When this matter is over, I will surrender myself to the law.
But now, there is something to be done, and a meeting to be had.
A Discipline and Supervision Censor colluding with a Judicial Review Office Deputy Minister in revolt... such a preposterous scene is a first for me.
If this is preposterous enough, then allow me to inconsequentially join the fray as well.
I trust you've been well, Drillmaster?
Taihe?! Were you not gravely wounded at Yumen—
Yes... my injuries were quite serious. I'm unsure if my skills are even one-third as powerful as they used to be.
Which is why I hope there will be no cause for me to make any moves today.
Why would even you participate in this...?

I have yet to grasp the full picture as to why Deputy Minister Lin has suddenly appeared here. But the two of you are my most respected colleagues, so I can only ask, as an old friend, that you two leave some space for negotiation.
How can you expect me to turn a blind eye when you lot insist on doing such a traitorous act?
'Loyalty over righteousness'... But whoever said you could only choose one from the two?
Righteousness...

lines.st: You're familiar with the case, and the two individuals involved in it stand here before you now.
Right or wrong, life or death. The choice is yours.
But Shifu... this matter came to be from complicated causes, how can what is good or evil be determined so easily—
The blade is in your hand. Make your choice!
Shifu...!
You hesitate still?!
Shifu, if 'righteousness' can only be upheld with the edge of my blade, then what sets it apart from 'violence'?
I don't understand...

lines.x: Stop dallying and go inside!
Don't even think about—
A signal to lower the Forbidden City's defenses...?
Could it be... His Imperial Majesty?
You can see the Forbidden City from here.
It was still spring when I last set foot in this tower.
Much has happened within the last six months.
The chill has come, and the wind atop the city walls is strong. Do take care of yourself, Grand Tutor.
I imagine only a few from the court have seen Baizao from this angle.
Even from great heights, where the view stretches far, one's sight will sometimes be obscured by clouds.
...All the more reason that one should see and think before one acts.
Do you know why I asked you to become Advisor of Yumen?
My thoughts were that you wanted me to broaden my horizons and see the big picture. An opportunity, but also a test.
A test for what exactly?
To see if I am worthy.
So you know why I have summoned you here today?
Yes, Grand Tutor. You are disappointed in me.
An excuse, or a genuine emotion?
I understand what I did during the Feast of a Hundred Delicacies was a terrible idea. But there was no other way.
Suspending the Feast will prevent the Grand Commandant from acting in drastic haste, and also allow the True Lung an opportunity.
Since your involvement in this slew of disputes, thrice have you been forced to make a choice, and each time you cast the riskiest die.
You placed your friends and even yourself onto the chessboard... have you never once felt hesitation?
I dare not claim it never crossed my mind... but I have always considered the choice most beneficial for all.
...A capable plotter, but the same cannot be said for your decision-making.

My plan was to select a young talent—ambitious but not greedy, resourceful but selfless—and give them many chances to hone themselves. If the chance arose, I would entrust them with significant responsibilities.
I thought you were a suitable candidate... but it often does not end well when a chess player uses himself as a piece on the board.
You remind me of a colleague... who is no longer with us.
I am humbled...
...Liang Xun, given another chance, would you willingly leave Shangshu?
When I started my studies, I was determined to apply my life's learning to the benefit of the people, regardless of whatever position I had gotten from mere chance...
My original ambition remains, and I hold no regrets over the choices I made.
Hm...
The incident at the Feast of a Hundred Delicacies cannot go unpunished.
I will submit myself to the law.
The Sky Mirror Pavilion's archive is lacking a transcriber, you will begin your duties there tomorrow morning.
You have my gratitude, Grand Tutor.
What is this...?
The time has come.
The clouds concentrate, completely obscuring the moonlight.
Have you met this one before...?
Never.
Who exactly... is the opponent he seeks on this weiqi board...?
There are times when one seeks not an opponent to win against on the board, but a solution.
A solution beneficial to both parties amid countless changes to the situation.
There is something I wish to consult you on...
What made you set up that exam in the first place?
Shop's not closed yet, is it?
You've come at the right time, I'll close right after I'm done with your order. It's late, so we don't have much left... but what would you like?
Anything to fill up my stomach on the road will do.
We happen to have half a steamer of meat buns here from the morning. You can have it for free.
Won't you be making a loss like this?
I only know how to cook, not so good at business stuff.
Why not earn a name and riches with skills like yours?
What for? Both nobles and peasants eat the same three meals a day. I might as well make more buns than money or fame.
If you don't mind another question, how much does a meal normally cost here?
Not much at all. The usual soup and two dishes just cost a few coins.
Anyone who works here in Baizao can eat their fill here.
I see you have a kind heart and compassion for others.
Aw, stop that... I'm just a chef who serves about a hundred customers a day at most.
I can only count on you to feed and clothe everyone else in this realm.
A free meal, and a lesson to boot... Lucky me.
Haha, I just hope you don't get mad at me. I just like aimlessly chatting with customers about everything under the sun.
Your restaurant has been here a long time, if I'm not wrong. How are your finances?
Aiya... Now that's a headache. You see, this little store of mine can barely make even.
It's all thanks to the abundant crops from Dahuang and the fast deliveries from the Chidao that I can keep my store running. Otherwise, I'd have to raise the prices.
Are you sure you're not trying to give credit to your siblings?
Haha... but it doesn't stop here.
My customers all live nearby. Some don't have enough coins, so they make up for it by sending me a few boxes of fresh fruit, or helping me repair the furniture...
Without their care, this restaurant wouldn't be running today.
So, you see... It's hard to balance a book like this.
But this is how you do business, right? Who says one side has to profit all the time? You give and you take. It's mutual prosperity that brings wealth and keeps the business thriving.
Your meat buns are ready and all packed, Grand Tutor. But why don't you try one now?
Not bad at all.
A word of advice. Don't take any more lessons from your businessman brother.
Were you able to get a meal, sir?
Yes. I heard the buns from this place are the best in Baizao. Try one yourself.
Uh... hm... wow, it's delicious.
Alright, I'm ready to get back on the road. Where to?
Shangshu...

lines.st: ...How do I get there again?
The seven streets and five lanes in the east of the city... I remember having to pass a dye workshop and a kiln first...
*sigh* My head hurts. It's been so many years; I wonder what changes—Oh? This calligraphy and painting store is still here? Surely their store sign can nearly be considered a historical relic...
I could come back again for a stroll. Who knows, I might just find it—on second thought, I probably couldn't if I tried.
Phew, almost there...
This ancient temple hasn't changed at all. No one's been here in years, but it hasn't been torn down either... Just who is feeling nostalgic for it?
The lock's already opened?
Oh, you're back.

lines.x: Why should anyone be born destined to bear all this?
I remember when Father was alive, he often stood atop the palace tower, my brother beside him.
Father often said this: Water can carry a boat, but also sink it. Likewise, this great height where we stand can also be the start of a plunge into the abyss.
He used to watch the city before him for long periods of time, or perhaps it was the vast territory in the distance—
He watched, both what he could and could not see.
Before my eyes, the city flourished, while his body gradually bent into an arc and his hair turned white as snow.
And I knew he was old.
Then, I began hearing all sorts of voices.
'The True Lung is an authoritarian dictator who has imposed many harsh policies! Great Yan will be in trouble if this continues for long!'
'Your Highness, please put the country first and admonish the True Lung, abolish his harmful policies, and bring a new start to this realm!'
The people are uneasy.
I should do something...
But what should I do? Who can I trust?
I thought of someone. A 'person' who is rumored to have fathomed every plan possible.
That ancient temple had long been deserted, but I tried everything I could and located a black stone.

lines.st: 'What do you want?'
'A solution. For Yan, and my elder brother.'
'And why should I help you?'
'Help me now, and in time, I will spare your life.'
'How amusing... You are bold, young Lung.'
'A solution is not difficult, but it comes with a price... Are you willing to pay that price?'

lines.x: With the Grand Preceptor's help, Brother ended up entering the Forbidden City with Chi Xiao in hand.
The Six Armies conceded, no one dared stop him.
No one knows what exactly happened inside the Forbidden City then.
Father died.
Brother vanished.
Why?
Am I to be alone now?
What is the meaning of this bowl of noodles?
I made some longevity noodles just for you. Aren't you going to try it?
Why?
Because today's your birthday, right?
A little squirt told me the True Lung's birthday is a day after the Mid-Autumn Festival, but he celebrates his birthday together with the festival out of humility—
I know a little something about hiding your birthday myself.
I just thought it was so sad for a man of such high status to not get to decide when to celebrate his own birthday.
You...
I know you...
Making your way here despite the odds, the risks, and even the possibility of losing your life...
You must have something to say to me.
Of course.
To think that my life is so closely connected to someone I haven't even met before. That feeling is just so surreal...
So I had to come and meet you.
I was planning to offer this to you yesterday during the Feast, but a whole bunch of things messed up my plan.
Now's a good time as any though.
I know it's Yanese custom to discuss important matters over a meal.
So dig in. When you're done, I have a load of questions to ask—
And I want answers.
*cough* *cough*... Pah, there's so much dust on the table I almost ate some by accident...
You...?
It's me. What's with the attitude? Not happy to see me?
I didn't think you would be the last one to get in my way.
Get in your way? What for? I've got better things to do.
I just want my worrisome brother who hasn't been home in a long time to have a proper meal.
After a century, the first thing you do is return to this run-down temple. Do you feel like you haven't been locked up enough here—or do you actually like it here?
I started this round of weiqi here, and likewise, I will end it in this temple—
All right. But first, clear up the table. There's no space for a bowl.
Why?
To eat, of course. What else?
You know what I'm planning to do?
Whatever it is, you shouldn't do it on an empty stomach.
It should have been a quiet night.
The faint rustling of armor echoes from afar, evoking the image of cold blades glinting silver under the moon. The crisp cries of startled fowlbeasts only deepen the temple's solitude.
Those outside remain still, awaiting orders and waiting for the one inside to come up with an answer.
What a show you've put on, with so many people for just one meal.
Moons on the fifteenth are fullest on the sixteenth, as they say. So we can still have a reunion dinner today, even if we missed our chance yesterday.
There's no need to rush, so why not finish this meal before you go, Wang, my second eldest brother?
Is Your Imperial Majesty surprised to see a woman who shouldn't exist...?
No...
I looked for you, for a long, long time...
I just didn't think you'd be taken so far away, hidden so deep.
I only found out yesterday that I'm still alive because of a heavy price that someone paid for me.
I'm afraid the story will be hard to imagine for Your Imperial Majesty.
But you know, I made it in here without much trouble. Must have been your doing.
So... you probably have something to tell me too, right?
Your very appearance here is itself the answer I seek.
I know you have many questions...
Ask them. Whatever your questions, I shall permit them...
That so...? I thought my mere presence would have angered you.
To be honest, you're quite different from what I expected.
I thought the True Lung would be like the rumors said, imposing, indifferent, impersonal.
How rude.
You know well to whom you speak, at this very moment?
To be honest, not really. I mean, two weeks ago you were still just someone from the stories.
When I was little, my dad told me lots of Yanese stories—historical classics, myths, and legends. But no matter what, he always spoke of a wise and strong emperor.
An emperor who toiled for his people.
He had to make major decisions for the whole nation, every decree issued was in the name of absolute justice. Great Yan thrived under his governance.
Sometimes I'd ask him, why not go back if you liked it so much? I was curious what this Yan he always talked about was actually like.
After all that curiosity, I got to tour around Baizao these past few days. I saw a flourishing nomadic city, where most work and live in peace and contentment...
So I figured, his stories were true, and you're probably a good ruler.
Your father...
You probably don't remember him. He was just a nameless nobody to you. My guess is even the dish that offended you a decade ago isn't worth remembering.
He wasn't actually my real father, even. Just acted out of his so-called 'morals', and saved a baby he thought was innocent.
So I think he was probably a good man too.
Gu Quan...

lines.st: I remember.
I remember it all.

lines.x: Actually, my dad talked about another Yanese too... someone who was a teacher to many.
He said that she devoted herself to the country to better everyone's lives, giving many scholars from humble backgrounds like him a chance to study.
After I arrived in Baizao, I heard a lot of stories and found out she wasn't someone from a fairytale. Yan did indeed have someone like her.
I found out... she was actually my relative.
Your Imperial Majesty. Could you tell me her story?
She was one of the Three Grand Ministers. Admired by all, stern and unyielding, a timeless role model... the Grand Preceptor.
She was the wisest and most learned erudite in Yan. Most imperial decrees issued in court at the time were based on her judgments.
She was a teacher to many, including me.
A teacher to many...
My dad said that although he was only her student for a few days, he was fortunate to have benefited from her 'wise words' and remains forever in debt to them.

It's an ancient Yanese saying. I can't recall it exactly, but it basically means everyone has their own standards of right and wrong, so there's no universal definition. Despite this, people should still follow what's right in their hearts.
'In worldly turmoil we tread, in our own conscience we trust'.
I remember it... she said it to me as well.
Which brings me to my next question: As your teacher, what did she teach you?
How to conduct myself as a person, as a minister, as a ruler.
Sounds complicated. What are the differences between the three?
As a person, to exercise decency and virtue, and keep the house in order; As a minister, demonstrate utmost loyalty and concern to the nation and people.
As a ruler... Always put the people before yourself, and strive to govern wisely...
Make decisions for the sake of the realm, my words weigh heavy, and can never be rescinded.
Can I understand that as: You have all the power in your position, but you lose the right to admit any mistakes?
True Lung of Yan, I want to ask you this.
How did such a good person turn into a 'scheming traitor' in everyone's eyes?
Do you think she was falsely accused?
No...

lines.st: No.
This is wrong.
Was she falsely accused?
It was not the answer, but a solution...

lines.x: Sacrifice the life of one, so that the entire nation can be stabilized, and its people pacified—
True Lung, how do the noodles taste?
Why?
I hear it's hard to get even good food down when you're feeling bad over something.
Do you have regrets?
*huff*... *huff*...
I heard... you were here.
Grand... Tutor...
I have an urgent... matter to report...
...Zuo Le.
I just consolidated the survey data from the Feranmutologist who has just returned from outside Yan.
In the northeast, north, and west of Yan, a total of five Feranmuts have shown signs of awakening.
Currently, this behavior cannot be explained by pure coincidence.
Although it is currently unknown how this relates to the convict's plot... if we start a war against the bestial Sui now, I fear the situation may spiral out of control!
You don't seem surprised at all... Was this within your calculations as well?
...He has been scheming for a century. This is but one of his plots.
After all that he has done, it cannot be mere mutual destruction he seeks.
But he's in Baizao right now, only one step away from the Tomb of Sui. If he adamantly presses on...
If he does, then Yan will not hesitate either.
...Was the capital like this also, a hundred and twenty years ago?
The convict had sneaked into the Tomb of Sui in a futile attempt to eliminate It by himself. This resulted in the death of a proxy, and the bestial Sui began to stir.
I looked through the Sui Regulator archives... but I never managed to figure out how he entered the mausoleum in the first place.
Just who... helped him enter the Tomb of Sui?
Why haven't you touched your chopsticks? Don't feel like eating?
A worthless move. A waste of time.
I know you're in a hurry, but I'm sure you can squeeze a meal in at least. Sheesh... I didn't even complain about the time it took to make all these.
Do you know how long I spent preparing this pot of soup?
Tell me.
If we start counting from the moment you left... About a century? Even longer if I include the time I spent sourcing ingredients and figuring out what to cook.
Have you found the 'flavor' you were searching for, after all this time spent in preparation?
No, I haven't. How could it be that easy?
But I came to understand something.
There is no perfect dish in this realm.
No matter how long I let this pot of soup stew, there's no way I could include every single flavor there is in it. It's just one dish.
Even a banquet spread of myriad delicacies will leave some unsatisfied.
But so what? People will always think about what to eat for their next meal. Saving what they didn't get for next time is what gives life hope, what keeps life going.
I remember a phrase Jie, my third eldest sister, taught me... 'What is truly complete, becomes incomplete'. That should be the shorter version of what I just said, right?
'Water fills, then spills. Moons wax, then wane'... Wang, do you still remember what Jie meant when she gave you your true name in one lone word?
...You came here to convince me.
With all I've said, am I not also convincing myself...?
When I walked out of that tomb, I couldn't tell the difference between how a 'human' should live, compared to a 'beast'.
I watched the people around me—both nobles and peasants alike. No matter the time or age, the people in this realm are mostly unhappy, miserable.

Yes, this land is flooded with suffering. Mortality is inevitable, meetings are fickle, requests go unfulfilled, and love will leave one day... what happiness and joy there is seems so minuscule in comparison.
Humans seek happiness and satisfaction, but they are no gods from the legends with their remarkable powers. Most times they find themselves helpless, powerless...
My curiosity continued. What's the point of living when there's so much hardship everywhere?
Only after a long time did I come to discover...
There is no happiness without sadness. How can one feel the joy of birth without knowing the grief of death? How can one rejoice over a meeting without experiencing the reluctance of separation?
I finally realized... every taste in this realm, begins from the flavor known as 'regret'.
The man silently picks up the pair of chopsticks, and slowly uses them to put food into his mouth.
Warmth spreads from the tip of his tongue to the rest of his body, and he cannot help but feel a little moved.
Wang... do you still remember?
You had just returned from the northern border with your troops. I heard you just were back fresh from a fierce battle.
I'd just opened my restaurant in Baizao then. Jie was also in town for some errands, so the three of us enjoyed a rare dinner together.
I still remember, Jie made an exception and drank several cups of wine. She imitated our eldest sister's drunken posture, writing poems on the wall. I never saw you so happy before.
It wasn't until midnight that the dinner ended. I was so tired I fell asleep at the table. The next day I awoke in bed, the two of you nowhere to be seen.
I was happy. It was the first time you had a meal with us, but I didn't think it would also be the last...
After that, we never met up again... neither you, nor Jie...
What exactly do you mean to say...?
At first, I didn't understand why humans insisted on having meals together.
Afterward, I slowly realized... A will to eat well also means a will to live well.
People who eat together, can help each other, and live on together...
It's hard... but I've always been hoping to eat together as a 'family' again.
I've imagined many times of how my big brothers and sisters would come back to Baizao one day.
Maybe Nian and Dusk would squabble on one side of the table. Shu would bring back some fresh greens, while Ji would show off some mysterious treasures he'd gotten from who knows where.
Maybe you're sitting together with our eldest brother and sister, talking about the view from a frontier fortress I've never seen, and the strange stories that happened over there.
I know our eldest brother is busy with official duties, and our eldest sister seems so freewheeling, but they're always thinking about their younger siblings... same as you.
I remember all your favorite flavors. I racked my brain thinking about what dishes to make for our feast.
Even if I knew deep down the day might just never come...
But I still keep on hoping. Because this is what keeps me from feeling hollow inside. It's what keeps my life going.
You know well it's an impossibility...
Harboring these unrealistic ideals will only increase your burden.
I know, but deep inside I just can't stop thinking about it... it's just human nature to do that.
To be honest, I miss Jie pretty deeply too.
When she vanished, I spent every day thinking about her.
I would think about her when I woke up, when I prepared meals... I'd remember how she taught me to read and write, and how she'd taste my cooking... And every time I would cry.
That was when I learned how eternal separation tastes to humans.
Of course... I know you feel the same.
So... the regret and pain inside you... I can sympathize too.
Sympathize...? How exactly do you plan on sympathizing with what I've gone through?
You forget, we are all 'I'!
I know you hate how the Sui Regulator watched Jie die, you hate our eldest brother because he stopped you from taking revenge, you even hated Jie... resented her for paying the price on your behalf.
But... the one you hate most, is yourself...
I know you, Wang. You're off to do something you have to do, and there's no way I can convince you to give it up...
I just want to say... you don't need to go in there ready to die. I can't bear to lose any of you...
Ji gave you a brocade, Shu gave you seeds... the only thing I can give you is this bowl of soup.
What is this...?
It's a 'power' I've never used before. I combined it with everything I've seen and experienced over a hundred years outside the tomb to make this bowl of soup.
Just take it as a helping from Yu to you...
Wang, my brother, I really... want you to come back safe...
The haggard-looking man is silent for a long time.
He raises his hand, as if wanting to wipe the tears off the young man's face, but ultimately, his arm drops back down feebly.
I didn't want you to get involved in this. You, especially.
You've forgotten who you are, forgotten the difference between man and beast, and even forgotten the answer you sought... which is fine.
You could have just lived on as you were...
So why... why did you remember, and come here?
It's fine if I forget who I am, but I can never forget the flavors deep inside... the feelings for the people I care about.
You have all dwelled in the mortal realm for too long and resemble humans too much.
But in the end, it is a mere 'resemble'.
I will help all of you...

lines.st: I will help all of you smash these fetters open.
I will help all of you find a place on this land.

lines.x: I will help all of you, become human.
Brother...?
The man puts down his chopsticks and bowl. He stands up and walks outside.
It was only later that I realized what the 'price' he spoke of truly meant.
It was a consequence I had never imagined before, even though my elder brother with the Grand Preceptor had long foreseen this outcome—or perhaps it was a way out.
Ridding a guilty Grand Preceptor and leaving a legitimate and innocent prince to succeed to the throne was the best result Yan could have, and also the choice they expected me to make.
Which I did...
But what for?
Is this the only way? Through a path paved with lies?
Is this fear?
Am I afraid of making a misstep, and stirring unrest in the state?
Afraid that history will inscribe my name with infamy for eternity?
Is this resentment?
Do I resent her for causing me to lose all that I had? Resent him for not staying behind, to face all of this together?
Or do I resent myself, for not doing more...?
Why am I feeling like this... how long have I not tasted such 'flavors'?
...Is this regret?
You know well it was a critical time...
The True Lung had just died from an unknown cause. The court was in panic, and powerful foes were waiting just outside...
If the situation hadn't been stabilized in time, the bloodshed would have been more than a thousandfold greater.
No, you don't understand. How could you...?
It's true, I don't...
All these considerations you've mentioned are leagues away from me. I couldn't even begin to imagine what it feels like, being responsible for the fate of so many people.
The most noble sacrifice I've seen was just a few idiots who shed all their blood on the battlefield for their ideals...
But Your Imperial Majesty... you definitely know it's a lie, convincing the masses with this 'explanation'.
A lie that happened just because the people will not allow the True Lung to make a mistake.
The Yan under your rule has so much land, so vast it can accommodate a hundred nomadic cities, millions of people...
So why couldn't it hold one more kindhearted person?!
Don't you feel... any remorse for her?

lines.st: What choice should I make? What choices did I even have?
How do I explain these principles to the one before me?
No... why should I even tell her?
Why did I... meet her...
What did I... want to hear from her?

lines.x: Well... I get it.
It's not a choice you'd make alone, when considering the so-called big picture.
Truth is not always power, when public opinion can triumph. Everyone sees and believes in different things...
The so-called truth only holds true to whoever claims it first.
My dad spent his whole life trying to find out the truth, but he chose to accept this.
It's not because he forgave you... he just agreed with the result you and that Grand Preceptor wanted to achieve together.
The occupant of the dragon throne puts down his chopsticks, and a light, faint sigh echoes throughout the great hall.
How do you want me... to compensate for this?
Compensate? No... that's impossible.
I know... to you and Yan, the reputation of one or the fates of a few are not even worth mentioning.
But for those unfortunate souls swept away by your grand plan, their suffering can't be undone.
Then, how do you want...?
Yeah, well... I was thinking about it before I came here. What kind of outcome do I even want?
An apology from you? To clear their names?
But is that enough comfort to those who are gone? Does it mean anything to those left behind?
I even considered the worst outcome of our meeting...
A ball of flames ignites and extinguishes within Blaze's palm, illuminating the palace for just a moment.
I just want you to promise me one thing.
Don't forget them.
I said before, there is no need to wait on me here...
Your Imperial Majesty's safety is paramount, so I beg your pardon for our defiance against your rule.
The traitor's descendant wantonly barged into the Forbidden City. It was a merciful act to grant her an audience.
Shall we charge her on grounds of deceiving Your Majesty?
News from the Sui Regulator. The convict has returned to the ancient temple.
Both Tianshis and Imperial Guardsmen are already in position. Yumen is also ready for battle.
Shall we give the order to slay him at once?
Pass on my decree...
Leizi!
You...
Heh... Good to see you alive.
What happened to you? Your face's all black and blue. Got your ass whooped?
Pah! You're one to talk! I can feel your hand trembling!
Are you scared...?
Yeah, I'm scared!
That's the True Lung after all! The king, the boss of a whole country! Just one word from him and many heads will roll!
I'm not scared of dying! I've been on the battlefield after all. I've been in shit more dire than this!
I'm just scared... that my meeting with the True Lung would be fruitless, wasting the opportunity everyone fought so hard for.
You idiot...
Then, tell me quick... what did you... talk about with the True Lung?
I... asked him for an explanation—For my dad, my younger sister, the relative I never met.
Hmph. I couldn't see his face clearly, but I'm sure he felt—
What's wrong?
...It's snowing?
The two are in such a hurry they only notice now the surrounding air has gradually gotten colder.
The energy poles scattered throughout the city gradually power down. It is mid-autumn, but the temperature plummets as if it's winter.
In contrast to the dimming city lights, the twin moons shine ever brighter.
Snow starts to fall.
Time's almost up.
We should go... Deputy Minister Yu.
Put it on me.
But...
Deputy Minister Yu, we are all aware of the man you are... so why not—
'All criminals should walk with a cangue and chains; even suspects should be clapped in irons'... And this is how you conduct yourself as a warden?
Put it on me!
As you say. Forgive my dereliction of duty.
Go slow... Let us savor this moonlight.
A snowflake floats through a window, landing on the prisoner's face.
What the...? Weather like this in the middle of autumn, and we even have snowflakes...?
(Gu Quan... I've done what I promised you, brother.)
Ning Yin is back in the study.
She takes out pen and paper along with an incomplete history book manuscript.
The story of the past, the hidden names... she knows them now and should be able to finish the final chapter.
Yet, she hesitates, her pen still.
A gust of wind flings the window open, and shuts the book.
A single snowflake lands on her draft, making the first stroke.
What...? It's snowing?
Blaze...
Is your shopkeeper still not here?
He... he's really not here this time...
It's fine. I guess I'm bound to never meet him.
I vowed to show everyone a Feast of a Hundred Delicacies never before seen, but it turned out to be a joke in the end.
I'm a joke myself as well.
I'm ready to let go, be it Ding Feng Lou or my culinary skills.
Chef Mo... He told me to give this to you when you showed up looking for him.
He knew I would come?
Jiang brings out an empty bowl, where a wooden plate lies within. Carved on it, in firm, strong strokes, are four Yanese characters—
'Tian Xia Di Yi'—'First Under Heaven.'
I was told, this title weighs heavy, but it cannot feed the hungry.
He also said that while culinary skills can be compared, hunger treats kings and paupers alike.
And that it didn't matter if there would be future top chefs, or just common cooks among those people on that snowy night; he would've made that pot of noodles for them anyway.
The old man is struck dumb, and then immediately bursts into hearty laughter.
Snow buffets his face. He has not felt this awake or elated for a long time.
...Hmph.
Heed the True Lung's decree.
Mobilize seven-tenths of the city's energy...
...To open the Tomb of Sui.
Yes.
The Feranmuts are also the citizens of Yan, no different than humans.
And if my citizen has an injustice to appeal, I will grant him permission.
Should he succeed, he would render Yan a meritorious service by ridding this thousand-year concern once and for all.
If he fails and the bestial Sui awakens, then he shall have the full might of Yan behind him. Together, we shall slay the beast, until the threat is no more.
Your Imperial Majesty, do you intend to pardon the convict?
Yes...

lines.st: There are many more I should pardon.
I pardon the convict Feranmut Wang for the crimes of manipulating the court and deceiving the emperor.
I pardon the Grand Preceptor for the crimes of false counsel, self-deprecation, and treason.
I pardon the deposed imperial heir Yan Wu for the crimes of dereliction of duty, abandoning his brother, and failing to uphold righteousness.
As for myself...
Failing to discern right from wrong, bringing injustice upon the good; failing to prevent war, leading to the deaths of many.
Crimes so heinous a pardon is impossible.
Though unworthy of the throne I occupy, I must do what I can.
I will ensure Yan’s fate belongs solely to its people.
I will settle my debt to this nation in full.
And when that day comes, I will await judgment for my crimes.
Yan Wu, it will be time for you to return.

lines.x: The night sky remains clear. Only a few thin clouds float above, the twin moons unobstructed. The wind delivers a chill at a perfect temperature.
Passers-by stop in their tracks to watch the inexplicable sight.
So much snow.
What... What's going on here?
Baizao's energy poles are reducing their power. It seems the energy is being redirected elsewhere.
The city has not seen snow for many years.
I see...
Blaze recalls when she entered the city. Her eyes were drawn to these innumerable energy poles that seemed to never dim.
She remembers what Xingzhu had told her, Baizao's magnificent founding history.
This city, this vast nation, history, and time—they have never stopped for anyone.
Until now...
Lin Qingyan, doesn't snow that falls out of season carry special meaning in Yanese stories?
What are you trying to imply? The truth is now revealed with white? Heaven sends snow to finally right a long-standing injustice? The typical snow in summer story?
Maybe... or something else.
Those who are gone—what they leave behind will eventually disappear, just like the snow here.
You...
I know...
I hope this snow will last a while longer.
Fully-armed armored soldiers line up both sides of the path, spanning across the entire city.
The man drags himself through a long black corridor.
With every step he takes, he feels that ancient, enormous existence responding to his visitation.
An angry roar, a hoarse howl.
Hoo...
Oh, my head... This doesn't feel like the usual hangover from the Husong wine I had last night.
Alas. I see.
It awakens from Its farcical dream... an inevitability.
Fine. Let's go have a look after this cup.
Let's see what scenes await at the end of this millennium-long dream.
The poet looks into the distance.
Clouds screen the sun, the realm obscured.
Who? Who was it?!
Professional work should be left to actual pros, who the heck messed with my adjustments—
Zip it! I've been here the entire time, and no one's come.
I sneaked out for just two rounds of mahjong with the Nongye Tianshis next door. How did this Heart get 'hypertension' again?
Do you still not get it?
I'm afraid... they've arrived.
Arrived?
Would those old fellows at Baizao let him go in that easily?
Did you forget? A century ago, he walked into the tomb the exact same way. Afterward, Jie vanished... and we still don't know what even happened.
This time...
Dusk... you scared?
Yes, I am.
This is it. Only someone without a heart or brain wouldn't be afraid.
I have no intention to just vanish.
Ooh, it's rare we agree on something.
Then what are we waiting for? Let's hurry before those old bones realize what we're up to.
You want to...?
With how things are, there's no way we're just going to sit here and wait to die. The Sui Regulator is the least of our concerns right now.
Let's go back 'home'... and hold a real family reunion.
So it's you...
I heard your voice. It's been a long time.
Ssh, I know you're in a bad mood. I can feel it too.
*sigh*... I mean, I spent the longest time in the tomb after all. I probably know you better than the others.
Strange. We're all 'I', so what's the point of going into this 'one side must perish' situation?
There's no explaining it, so I can only reluctantly accept it.
Hmph... You're strong, but I don't think Wang—I don't think we will lose to you.
You've slept for a thousand years after all, while we've spent that time living real lives—so how can we lose, right?
I can't use the power I took from you... so take it back. Consider it a farewell from me.
There's no avoiding this outcome, so we might as well treat it as a salvation.
Sleep...
A stove fire is lit. It is a warm flame, harboring life and hope, enough to dispel the chill of the winter night and the anxiety in the people's hearts.
The restless ancient behemoth far away returns to calm yet again.
Moonlight fills the city, and every dreamer dreams a good dream.

lines.st: Seeking an answer, he feels lost,
Clarity he finds in worldly flavors,
His life is a feast, an eternity aflame.

lines.x: It's gotten quiet inside...? I suppose our youngest brother's soup was timely...
We ended up troubling him a little.
Was your visiting him earlier not part of your plan?
Truly, nothing gets past you...
This is it... shall we go in together?
No...
Hand me the items.
With this blade... and what we got from our dear sister and youngest brother.
Our strength still pales in comparison, but at least the odds don't seem insurmountable.
What now?
Brother...?
This is well enough.
You've done what I've asked of you. What I promised you, you already have.
Our debt is settled. I will finish this myself.
What's that supposed to mean...?
Things are finally even... If we have it your way, won't I be in your debt again?
Leave, Ji...
What happens next, is between us.
I've been playing against me all along, in order to determine a winner.
We have long since ceased to be your vassals. Now we must fight, for victory or death.
Just like the battle a millennium ago, this too is inevitable.
Your arrogance, your indifference, it all stops here.
Are you angry? Afraid? Anticipating?

lines.st: Come.
Your move.

lines.x: ...What night is it?
A gloomy mausoleum sits in pitch-black silence. In the chaotic darkness, even time seems to come to a standstill.
How long has it been since the last sign of activity in here?
But on this day, a new voice echoes in the tomb.

lines.st: Where is this...?
A tomb.
Who am I?
Unknown.
...Who are you?
Also unknown.
Though... they did give me a name.
'Sui'.

lines.x: Have you been here for a long time?
If you would, Mr. Yu.
Before you take me away, might I make a presumptuous request?
If it's not too much trouble, could you go over to that small restaurant across the street, and buy me a bowl of plain rice with a cup of tea?
It's no good to be imprisoned on an empty stomach.
It's my job. Please wait a moment.
Are you going?
The sentence has been passed. I can't very well dally around the Judicial Review Office.
I know... Which is why I'm here today to send you off.
You don't seem to resent me.
Now that I know the whole truth, I find no reason to resent you.
Although, I could resent you for not telling me earlier.
If I did, I fear you might be clad in the same fetters as I have on me right now.
I had to leave a few worthy successors for the Judicial Review Office...
I've raised an objection to your conviction.
In truth, if you feel this unjust, you can request a review at the Three Organs of Justice—
Could you possibly think what I did is unworthy of a decade behind bars?
But all that you did was ultimately for...
What difference does it make?
The law is the law, not a mere guide to what is 'moral'.
If I refuse to comply with the law today, how many more like me will there be in the future, and how much chaos will they create in the name of 'morality'?
It is unlike a disciple of mine to be spouting such words.
I have been careless in my words...
It's up to young people like you, and those who follow to steer the 'law' closer to what many deem is right.
I'll be waiting for that day... And since that pine tree on the mountain still stands, I'll also be waiting for you to cut it down and craft me a coffin from its wood.
By the way, why have you come here today not dressed in the Judicial Review Office's uniform?
I resigned.
A thunderclap can illuminate a moment, but it cannot distinguish what is good or evil, true or false for everything. There are many things I cannot perceive clearly.
The pine tree remains on the mountain, but it is merely a tree. What the mountain climber sees in the tree, depends on their intentions.
I will return to the Tianshi Bureau and practice my craft for a while.
...Very well.
Doing something you don't understand with muddled eyes will never bring you any achievements.
Sir... there is still one thing I do not understand.
Why exactly... did you insist on investigating the Grand Preceptor's case?
Why do you ask?
I organized the files surrounding this case only to find Gu Quan was not the only one who sacrificed everything he had for this affair.
Gu Quan may have had his own motives, but you have no direct connection to the case, save for being under orders back then to bring this unresolved case at the Judicial Review Office to light.
Mr. Yu, the meal you wanted.
The waiter at Savors at Yu's refused to take payment, and even said they look forward to seeing you again.
Strange, I didn't even mention the meal was for you...
I recall meeting Gu Quan in a restaurant several decades ago, when he imparted this to me: 'Too many colors blind the eyes, too many flavors dull the tongue'...
I only understood his words later, probably owing to my short-sightedness and dull sense of taste.
My eyes see only the unvarnished truth, and my tongue savors only the unaltered taste.
The man clad in prisoner attire turns around and takes one last look at the plaque above the doors of the Judicial Review Office, and the courtroom behind the doors.
He bends down, scoops a handful of unmelted snow under a cypress tree, and sprinkles it onto his rice bowl. He finishes his meal in a few mouthfuls.
Tasty.
...Thus, I depart.

lines.st: Why are you here?
Because of a mistake... an oversight...
I can feel it. You're in so much pain, so much anger...
But after so long, your wound is healing, your rage, subsiding.
You sound... exhausted?
Yes...
My consciousness, my soul, it has split into multiples.
Just like you...
Where did they go?
Outside the tomb, they call it the 'mortal realm'.
The 'mortal realm'... Is it a beautiful place?
Not at all...
There I have witnessed the most savage slaughters, the most malicious plots, the most merciless betrayals!
Kin destroys kin, love turns to hate...
Arrogance caused my failure, because I could not comprehend 'human emotions'.
If the mortal realm is such a vicious and repulsive place...
Then why did they go there anyway?
Because...

lines.x: Who goes there?
Quite the distance you've hiked. Where you off to?
...Why does it concern you?
I'll tell you why. You caused a ruckus in Yan, and you expect me to do nothing?
You old things probably forgot the pain of a wound before it even healed. If we could beat you up and drive you out then, we can do it again now.
With just yourself? Laughable...
Oh, was that a challenge to a fight? Let's go.
I know you have a few worlds down in that belly of yours. Let's see who's stronger—you or that painter.
Let's see how long your leftover worlds can stand against my flames.
You will pay for your impertinence.
Hmph...
Forget it, there's no use in killing you. You'd only mold another body to life just like before.
If this is your excuse for backing down, I accept.
I can't be bothered to argue with you, but before I locate your true form and off you for good, there's a question I want to ask.
I'm curious: What exactly did that weiqi failure promise you to make you obey his orders so willingly?
No... Not just you, all of you.
His plots do not concern me. We only share some common goals.
The one I seek, is that disgraceful traitor imprisoned in a tomb.
If you only wanted revenge, you would've just killed Its proxies one by one, then wait for It to die in war with Yan.
So many years have passed. Your so-called kin on this land surely only numbers a few now... You're just feeling nostalgic.
This 'human emotion' is what makes you different than beasts.
Absurd.
No... nope, that can't be the only reason why you've conspired with him.
Don't think you can fool me. In all these years, Yan has always kept a few pairs of eyes on you.
Are you lot still thinking about that thing under Baizao...?
Uncle Lee...
Are we... gonna find Dad here?
This was the only place I got after asking around.
*sigh*... After so many years apart, it's the first time I was hoping news about him would turn out to be false.
But I can't think of anyone else who'd fit the bill: Someone so obsessed with martial arts that they've fought everyone worth fighting and even intentionally injured others on multiple occasions...
How did he end up like this...?
No clue...
His obsession is unmatched. Although... the stronger the obsession is, the harder it usually is for the mind to break.
What was that?!
Waai Fu... we'll find him soon, but be careful.
You might be the only one who can wake him from his madness.
Mm...
Let's go, Uncle Lee.
*sigh*...
(Was the blade falling into his hands truly a sleight of fate...?)
(Liang Xun, oh, Liang Xun... If something were to happen to Huai Tianpei, it'd be on your head.)
From a distance, a burly body can be vaguely seen in the depths of the cave.
Heavy fists repeatedly pound the rock walls, leaving blood stains that resemble plum blossoms. Every punch shakes the entire cave.
Only now do the visitors realize this 'cave' has been carved out by the punches.
Forty years... The First Under Heaven... when did I...
The blade... I can't give it away... throw... I better throw it away!
My daughter... I won't let you harm her!
Dad...?
Dad... wake up...
Dad!
You can almost see Baizao from here.
Visiting this posthouse has unexpectedly turned into a trip down memory lane.
What, you lot insisted on following me, so why the silence now?
All who died here are our brothers.
All for 'loyalty'...
Lord Wei. Someone's following us.
Mm...
Watch out—
Impressive swordplay...
In the many years since we last met, your swordplay has turned incomparable.
Lady Ch'en.
Long time no see... Wei Yenwu... Uncle.
Hui-chieh...
I thought what you did in Lungmen was the stupidest decision you could make...
You, and these men... what are your plans now, even?
You shouldn't be here.
Could it be Fumizuki...? She told you?
I made this decision myself. Nothing to do with anyone else.
This affair likewise has nothing to do with you—
...True.
I've never asked about your past. I considered it your private business, and felt I had no right to pry.
I've been to many places these past few years and seen many things I never saw or even thought of before.
I think I've come to understand that despite all the contradictions in this land, 'righteousness' still exists.
What happened to my mother... and my sister... You can't say they have nothing to do with your past.
So I want to see for myself how you plan to unravel this past you've been entangled in your entire life.

lines.st: I seem to suddenly remember many things.
Things I saw within an enormous body, through eyes that can see through the mist.
You are a part of this consciousness, so it is only natural you possess the same memories.
But... the mortal realm I saw is different from what you described.
So what did you see?
A fire.
At the very beginning, people congregated to fend off ferocious wildbeasts, and started a fire to light up the cold nights.
They gathered around the flames, forged all sorts of tools, and cooked all kinds of dishes. Their faces bore many expressions I cannot comprehend...
I really want to know, what those feelings are.
Such a thought, is very dangerous...
Dangerous?
Your kin, the proxies who are already roaming about the mortal realm, entered it with similar thoughts. But the only thing awaiting them was endless torture.
Did they all... want to become human?
Before I become one, first I want to know...
'What is humanity?'
Where should I go to look for my answer...?
Heh... just go.
Take a walk in the 'mortal realm'.
A cacophony of emotions and desires will bring endless suffering. Give a thought form, and it will resemble the dawn of chaos. Fall into its fetters, and you will never break free.
...You're not afraid?

lines.x: Ahhhh!!!!!
Huff... Oh, just a dream...
I guess it's not so strange to dream about It...
Where's everyone...
The young chef leaps off the couch, stretches himself, and rubs his eyes hard.
The kitchen before him remains the same, pots and pans all in place.
He seems to suddenly think of something, and hurriedly opens a cupboard, taking out bottles of oil, salt, soy sauce, and vinegar.
Carefully, he drips a drop from each bottle into his mouth one by one, his brows tightly knit, deep in thought.
Only after a long time does a smile appear on his face.
Phew... Thank goodness...
Still tastes the same...
Whether as man or beast, whether I remember or not... it doesn't seem to make much difference.
The flavors of the land are endless, and I've discovered all the ones I've been seeking. As for what comes next... Aiya, let's not think about that right now!
Enjoy the flavors of today and leave tomorrow's worries for tomorrow... That's all there is to it.
Let's get cooking.
